author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
rāgera bhajana⋅patha, kahi ebe abhimata,
loka veda⋅sāra ei vāṇī
sakhīra anugā hañā, vraje siddhadeha pāñā,
ei bhāve juḍābe parāṇī

śrī⋅rādhikāra sakhī jata, tāhā nā kahiba kata,
mukhya sakhī kariyā gaṇana
lalitā, viśākhā tathā, sucitrā, campakalatā,
raṅgadevī, sudevī kathana

tuṅgavidyā, indurekhā, ei aṣṭasakhī lekhā,
ebe kahi narma⋅sakhīgaṇa
iho sevā⋅sahacarī, ‘priya⋅preṣṭha’ nāma dhari’
prema⋅sevā kare anukṣaṇa

(samasnehā viṣama snehā, nā kariha dui lehā,
kahi mātra adhikasnehāgaṇa
nirantara thāke saṅge, kṛṣṇakathā līlāraṅge,
narmasakhī ei saba jana )
śrī⋅rasamañjarī āra, śrī⋅ratimañjarī sāra,
lavaṅgamañjarī, mañjunālī
lavaṅgamañjarī⋅saṅge, kastūrikā⋅ādi raṅge,
premasevā kare kutūhalī

e⋅sabāra anugā hañā, premasevā niba cāñā,
iṅgite bujhiba saba kāje
rūpe guṇe ḍagamagī, sadā haba anurāgī,
vasati kariba sakhāmājhe

vṛndāvane dui jana, cāridike sakhīgaṇa,
samayera sevā⋅rasa⋅sukhe
sakhīra iṅgita habe, cāmara ḍhulāba tabe,
tāmbūla jogāba cā̐da mukhe

jugala caraṇa sevi, nirantara ei bhāvi,
anurāge thākiba sadāya
sādhane bhāviba jāhā, siddha dehe pāba tāhā,
rāgapathera ei se upāya

sādhane je dhana cāi, siddhadehe tāhā pāi,
pakvāpakva mātra se vicāra
pākile je premabhakti, apakve ‘sādhana’ khyāti,
bhakati⋅lakṣaṇa⋅anusāra

narottama⋅dāsa kahe, ei jena mora haye,
vrajapure anurāge vāsa
sakhīgaṇa⋅gaṇanāte, āmāre gaṇibe tāte,
tavahu̐ pūriba abhilāṣa

tathāhi⋅
sakhīnāṁ saṅginīrūpāmātmānaṁ vāsanāmayīm
ājñāsevāparāṁ tattat kṛpālaṅkārabhūṣitām

kṛṣṇaṁ smaran janañcāsya preṣṭhaṁ nijasamīhitam
tattatkathārataścāsau kuryādvāsaṁ vraje sadā
en_translation:
I will now speak my opinion on the path of worship in rāga (spontaneous devotion); this is the essence of the words of the people and the Vedas. Becoming a follower of the sakhīs, attaining a siddha-deha in Vraja—in this way, the life will be soothed.

How many sakhīs of Śrī Rādhikā are there? I will not say how many. Counting the principal sakhīs: Lalitā, Viśākhā, and Sucitrā, Campakalatā, Raṅgadevī, Sudevī are mentioned.

Tuṅgavidyā, Indurekhā—these eight sakhīs are written. Now I speak of the narma-sakhīs. They are assistants in service, bearing the name 'Priya-preṣṭha'; they perform loving service at every moment.

(Sama-snehā, Viṣama-snehā—do not make affection for these two; I speak only of the Adhika-snehā group. They remain constantly in company, in Kṛṣṇa-kathā and the joy of pastimes; all these people are narma-sakhīs.)

Śrī Rūpa-mañjarī, and Śrī Rati-mañjarī—the essence; Lavaṅga-mañjarī, Mañjulālī. Along with Lavaṅga-mañjarī, with musk and so on, they enthusiastically perform loving service in joy.

Becoming a follower of all of them, I will ask for and take loving service; I will understand all tasks by hints. Overwhelmed by beauty and qualities, I will always be affectionate; I will reside in the midst of the sakhīs.

The Two Persons in Vṛndāvana, sakhīs on four sides, in the happiness of the rasa of timely service. There will be a hint from the sakhī, then I will wave the whisk; I will supply betel nuts to the moon-faces.

Serving the lotus feet of the Couple, constantly thinking this, I will always remain in affection. What I think of in sādhana, I will get that in the siddha-deha; this is the method of the path of rāga.

The wealth I want in sādhana, I get that in the siddha-deha; the consideration is only ripe and unripe. When ripe, it is prema-bhakti; in the unripe state, it is known as 'sādhana', according to the characteristic of bhakti.

Narottama Dāsa says: May this be mine—residence in Vrajapura in affection. In the counting of the sakhīs, they will count me in that; then my desire will be fulfilled.

Thus it is said: "One should meditate upon oneself in a form that is a female companion (sakhī) to the Divine Couple—a form born of spiritual desire, dedicated to following their commands and serving them, and adorned with the ornaments of their mercy. While remembering Kṛṣṇa and His beloved associates who are dear to one’s own heart, one should remain constantly absorbed in narrations about them and reside eternally in Vraja."
